title: celtis membranacea
intro: Celtis membranacea, also known as the netted bush plum, is a species of tree in the family Cannabaceae that is native to northern Australia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its leaves, bark, and fruit exhibiting medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Celtis membranacea include its use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as a decoction of the leaves and bark, or a tincture made from the fruit, which are then used to treat various health conditions.
